testing_centers.notes: In-person test centers worldwide and online proctored via OnVUE

id_requirements: Two forms of ID are required. Primary ID must be government-issued, include candidate's name, photo, and signature (e.g. passport, driver's license). Secondary ID must include candidate's name and signature or name and photo.
exam_rules: No notes, scratch paper, electronic devices, books, or study materials permitted. Personal items must be stored. No food or drink except water. Restroom breaks allowed but timer continues. Testing center staff may monitor via video/audio.
renewal_options: Earn CE credits through CompTIA CE program (training, conferences, publishing, teaching, higher certs) or retake the current exam version before expiry.
target_audience: security architects and senior security engineers
recommended_experience: minimum of 10 years of general hands-on IT experience, including 5 years of hands-on security, with Network+, Security+, CySA+, Cloud+, and PenTest+ or equivalent knowledge

prerequisites.description: Network+, Security+, CySA+, Cloud+, and PenTest+ or equivalent knowledge

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period required before first retake. After the second failed attempt, candidates must wait 14 calendar days before any subsequent attempt. No limit on total attempts.

objectives.title: Security program documentation
sub_objectives: policies, procedures, standards, and guidelines.

objectives.title: Program management
sub_objectives: training (phishing, security, privacy), communication, reporting, and RACI matrix.

objectives.title: Frameworks
sub_objectives: COBIT, ITIL, etc.

objectives.title: Configuration management
sub_objectives: asset life cycle, CMDB, and inventory.

objectives.title: GRC tools
sub_objectives: mapping, automation, and compliance tracking.

objectives.title: Data governance
sub_objectives: production, development, testing, and QA.

objectives.title: Risk management
sub_objectives: impact analysis, risk assessment (quantitative vs. qualitative), third-party risk, confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

objectives.title: Threat modeling
sub_objectives: actor characteristics, attack patterns, and frameworks (ATT&CK, CAPEC, STRIDE).

objectives.title: Attack surface
sub_objectives: architecture reviews, data flows, and trust boundaries.

objectives.title: Compliance strategies
sub_objectives: industry-specific standards (PCI DSS, ISO/IEC 27000).

objectives.title: Security frameworks
sub_objectives: NIST, CSF, CSA, and others.

objectives.title: Cloud capabilities
sub_objectives: CASB (API-based, proxy-based), shadow IT detection, shared responsibility model, CI/CD pipeline, Terraform, Ansible, container security, orchestration, and serverless workloads.

objectives.title: Cloud data security
sub_objectives: data exposure, leakage, remanence, insecure storage, and encryption keys.

objectives.title: Cloud control strategies
sub_objectives: proactive, detective, and preventative controls; customer-to-cloud connectivity, service integration, and continuous authorization.

objectives.title: Network architecture
sub_objectives: segmentation, microsegmentation, VPN, always-on VPN, and API integration.

objectives.title: Security boundaries
sub_objectives: asset identification, management, attestation, data perimeters, and secure zones.

objectives.title: Deperimeterization
sub_objectives: SASE, SD-WAN, and software-defined networking.

objectives.title: Zero trust concepts
sub_objectives: defining subject-object relationships.

objectives.title: Automation
sub_objectives: scripting (PowerShell, Bash, Python), event triggers, IaC, cloud APIs, generative AI, containerization, patching, SOAR, and workflow automation.

objectives.title: Vulnerability management
sub_objectives: scanning, reporting, and SCAP (OVAL, XCCDF, CPE, CVE, CVSS).

objectives.title: Advanced cryptography
sub_objectives: PQC, key stretching, homomorphic encryption, forward secrecy, and hardware acceleration.

objectives.title: Cryptographic use cases
sub_objectives: data at rest, in transit, and in use; secure email, blockchain, privacy, compliance, and certificate-based authentication.

objectives.title: Cryptographic techniques
sub_objectives: tokenization, code signing, cryptographic erase, digital signatures, hashing, and symmetric/asymmetric cryptography.

objectives.title: Monitoring and data analysis
sub_objectives: SIEM (event parsing, retention, false positives/negatives), aggregate analysis (correlation, prioritization, trends), and behavior baselines (network, systems, users).

objectives.title: Vulnerabilities and attack surface
sub_objectives: injection, XSS, insecure configurations, outdated software, and weak ciphers; mitigations include input validation, patching, encryption, and defense-in-depth.

objectives.title: Threat hunting
sub_objectives: internal intelligence (honeypots, UBA), external intelligence (OSINT, dark web, ISACs), TIPs, IoC sharing (STIX, TAXII), and rule-based languages (Sigma, YARA, Snort).

objectives.title: Incident response
sub_objectives: malware analysis (sandboxing, IoC extraction, code stylometry), reverse engineering, metadata analysis, data recovery, and root cause analysis.

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Learn
official_study_resources.notes: Official e-learning with performance-based questions and study guides

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Practice
official_study_resources.notes: Adaptive practice question engine with rationale
